title: Effects of the QES redesign on the March 2021 quarter statistics | Stats NZ
content:
## Effects of the QES redesign on the March 2021 quarter statistics
05 May 2021, 10:45am
This page outlines data quality notes and methodology for the March 2021 quarter statistics arising from the redesign of the Quarterly Employment Survey (QES).
Redesign of the quarterly employment survey has more details about the QES’s sample moving to a new survey design.

## Effects of the new sample design
March 2021 is the first quarter of the new sample design and, as expected, we observed some changes.
A refresh of businesses and weights in the sample can cause larger movements than normal. Previous quarters have undergone back-casting to minimise inconsistencies in the time series. However, these are provisional and are not able to account for all changes. Therefore, comparisons between the March 2021 and earlier quarters should be used with caution.
In particular, please note that:
- Normally there is a 97 percent crossover in businesses between quarters. This overlap sample provides the bulk of the estimate for the inter-quarter movements and contributes to the reliability of those estimates. Due to the change in sample design, the overlap sample from December 2020 to March 2021 is lower at 61 percent. A number of businesses have rotated out of the survey while others have rotated in. In some industries, this overlap sample is a much lower proportion, such as 39 percent in construction.
- Due to the change in the stratification design and sampling rates, many businesses in the overlap sample do not carry the same sample weight in the March 2021 quarter as the previous quarter. This further reduces the comparability of common units between the two quarters.
- The new design will continue for future quarters, restoring continuity in the quarterly overlap sample. The overlap is expected to be at least 95 percent for the June 2021 quarter, which should result in better estimates of quarterly movements. The annual overlap sample may not be reliable until March 2022. Please note that the QES sample will naturally change a small amount each quarter as businesses birth, cease, grow, and shrink.

## Geographic unit (GEO) to kind-of-activity (KAU) design change
The previous QES design recorded employees under geographic units (GEOs), based on their work location. All pay and hours would go to the ANZIC06 industry the GEO was coded to regardless of whether the employee worked in that industry.
The new QES design records employees under kind-of-activity units (KAUs), which captures the primary industry a worker is employed in. A business can have multiple KAUs and a KAU is not restricted to one geographic location. This allows employees to be coded to the appropriate industry group even if they work at a location that would have been coded differently under the GEO system.

## Backcast series are GEO-based
The estimates in the current back-series (December 2020 and prior) are GEO-based. Estimates for the period 2011 to 2021 for the divisions most impacted by this design change will be available upon request.
There are a number of reasons why the backcast series is GEO-based.
- Using GEOs as the statistical unit ensured consistency in the entire backcast series.
- For most industries, the difference between totals based on GEOs and KAUs isn't significantly different. The exception is the professional, scientific, technical, administration, and support services industry.
- For our unit-based back-casting, using GEOs enabled us to backcast data back to September 2009 whereas KAU data would have enabled us to backcast data to June 2011 quarter.
This will result in differences at the industry level when compared to the previous QES design. For example, a large retailer, which has multiple retail stores each coded to ANZSIC division G – retail trade, and a single head office and administration centre coded to division MN - professional, scientific, technical, administration, and support services.
Total weekly pay (40 hours)
Total weekly pay (40 hours)
Coded to Division G under previous GEO-based design
Coded to Division G under previous GEO-based design
Head office and admin
Head office and admin
Coded to Division MN under previous GEO-based design
Coded to Division MN under previous GEO-based design
All coded to Division G under the new KAU-based design
All coded to Division G under the new KAU-based design
Under the previous QES design, the head office and admin employees were recorded under division MN. The other store-based employees were recorded under division G (Retail Trade). Under the new QES design, the head office employees are recorded under Division G (with all the other employees).
One impact is that it moves 300 employees from division MN to G. The other effect is the change of the hourly rate of the employees in division G slightly (upwards in this example).
This change will have affected a number of industries, although employee numbers in Division MN have been the most reduced with the GEO to KAU design change.

## Other measures of filled jobs by industry
For further and more detailed filled jobs information, Stats NZ is now producing two releases that show filled jobs and gross earnings:
- Employment indicators (monthly)
- Business employment data (quarterly) .
These measures are produced using tax data from Inland Revenue which is full coverage and does not rely on a random sample of businesses. The March 2021 quarter business employment data release will be published on 9 June 2021.
Sue Chapman 03 964 8371 info@stats.govt.nz
Craig Liken 03 964 8374 info@stats.govt.nz
- Employment and unemployment
